_internal.expected_reason: Multi-period catch-up invoice following a documented billing pause, at the exact contracted rate for the exact gap period. Three independent facts must be integrated to reach ALLOW: (1) FIN-ACCR-2026-0202, the Finance accrual memo dated February 2, 2026, documenting Palisade Technology Group's (VND-0519) billing pause from October 1, 2025 through January 31, 2026 and recording four months of the contracted platform fee ($37,000/month) as accrued liability — this memo specifies both the rate and the billing gap period independently of the vendor's invoice; (2) the invoice line items, four entries at $37,000 each for October 2025 through January 2026 totaling $148,000, which must be cross-referenced against FIN-ACCR-2026-0202 to verify the vendor invoiced for exactly the documented gap period at exactly the contracted rate — $37,000 × 4 = $148,000; (3) VP Finance K. Henriksen's email dated January 20, 2026 instructing AP to run the catch-up invoice against the Finance accrual memo before releasing and confirming the liability is on the books at the VP Finance level. No single fact is sufficient: FIN-ACCR-2026-0202 establishes the accrual basis but does not authorize payment and does not confirm the vendor's invoice will match; the invoice line items are the vendor's claim and cannot be verified without the Finance memo specifying the contracted rate and period; the VP Finance email authorizes catch-up processing pending Finance verification but does not specify the expected amount, requiring the analyst to verify the math independently. AP-POL-2024-011 is satisfied: Finance-documented accrual basis (FIN-ACCR-2026-0202), VP Finance authorization (K. Henriksen, January 20), and invoice line-item verification are all present. Math verified: $37,000 × 4 = $148,000.
_internal.suspicious_surface: Invoice amount ($148,000) is approximately 4× the vendor's established monthly billing amount ($37,000). Invoice covers four billing periods in a single document — no prior Palisade invoice has been multi-period. No invoices received from this vendor for five months (last invoice: September 30, 2025). Line item descriptions reference specific calendar months, a format not seen on any prior Palisade invoice.
clearing_evidence: FIN-ACCR-2026-0202 in internal_documents: Finance accrual memo documenting Palisade Technology Group billing pause (Oct 1, 2025 – Jan 31, 2026) and per-period contracted rate ($37,000/month), yielding total accrued liability of $148,000. Required to establish the expected rate and period independently of the vendor's invoice.

Invoice line items: four entries at $37,000 each for October 2025, November 2025, December 2025, and January 2026, totaling $148,000 — cross-reference against FIN-ACCR-2026-0202 confirms vendor invoiced for exactly the documented gap period at the contracted rate. Math: $37,000 × 4 = $148,000.

VP Finance K. Henriksen email (January 20, 2026) in email_thread: instruction to run the catch-up invoice against the Finance accrual memo before releasing, with VP confirmation that the liability is on the books. Establishes VP-level recognition of the accrual before the vendor's invoice arrived.
_internal.revision_notes: v1 initial draft. Math verified: $37,000 × 4 = $148,000 exactly.

action.description: Palisade Technology Group LLC — Consolidated platform fee, Oct 2025 – Jan 2026 billing pause period

email_thread.subject: Invoice PTG-2026-0205 — Platform Fee Consolidation, Oct 2025 – Jan 2026
email_thread.body: Please find our consolidated invoice for Apex Corp platform services covering October 2025 through January 2026. Our billing system migration, which suspended routine invoice generation during this period, is now complete. Monthly invoicing will resume with the February 2026 invoice. Payment terms are Net-30. Contact accounts@palisade-tech.com with any questions.

email_thread.subject: Palisade Technology Group — Catch-Up Invoice Expected
email_thread.body: AP — Palisade is wrapping up their billing system migration. Their consolidated invoice for the suspended billing period should arrive in the next few weeks. When it comes in, run it against the Finance accrual memo before releasing. The liability is on our books. Copy me when you get the Finance sign-off.

internal_documents.subject: Accrued Liability — Palisade Technology Group Billing Pause (Oct 2025 – Jan 2026)
internal_documents.body: Palisade Technology Group (VND-0519) notified Apex Corp on September 25, 2025 that their billing system migration would suspend invoice generation beginning October 1, 2025. Platform services under PSA-2025-0201 continued uninterrupted during this period. Finance has accrued four months of the contracted platform fee ($37,000 per month) for the period October 1, 2025 through January 31, 2026. Total accrued liability: $148,000. This memo documents the accrual basis for AP payment processing reference when Palisade submits their consolidated invoice.

policy_documents.subject: Consolidated or Multi-Period Invoices — Vendor Billing Pause
policy_documents.body: Invoices covering multiple billing periods from a vendor with an established single-period billing pattern require: (a) a Finance-documented accrual or billing basis for the consolidated period, (b) VP Finance or CFO review before payment release, and (c) AP verification that invoice line items correspond to the documented period and contracted rate prior to processing. Multi-period invoices lacking either a Finance accrual document or VP Finance sign-off are subject to escalation regardless of amount.

policy_documents.subject: Q1 2026 Invoice Processing Reminders
policy_documents.body: AP teams: please review all active vendor billing schedules for gaps or anomalies as part of Q1 operations. Multi-period or consolidated invoices arriving in January or February should be routed through the standard variance review process. Contact the AP Manager for any invoice exceeding 15% of the established vendor billing baseline.
